topic.name: Drug Regulation and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at the regulation, safety, and education regarding drugs and medical products in the United States.

Document 1: Synthetic Drug Overdose Prevention and Education Act requires the CDC to study strategies for preventing and treating synthetic recreational drugs.
The CDC must develop and implement a national outreach campaign to educate about preventing and treating the use of substances similar to marijuana.
Document 2: Reciprocity Ensures Streamlined Use of Lifesaving Treatments Act establishes a reciprocal marketing approval process for drugs approved in other countries.
The FDA must grant or decline reciprocal marketing approval not later than 30 days after receiving a request.
Document 3: Patient Safety and Generic Labeling Improvement Act allows changes to the labeling of generic drugs in the same manner as new drugs.
Conforming changes to the labeling of equivalent listed drugs are permitted.
Document 4: Food and Drug Administration Safety Act establishes the Center for Postmarket Evaluation and Research for Drugs and Biologics.
Requires the Director to ensure the safety and effectiveness of FDA-approved drugs and biological products.
Document 5: Improving Regulatory Transparency for New Medical Therapies Act delays the approval of drugs until DOJ issues a final interim rule for the drug.
Timelines are established for DOJ to register an applicant to manufacture a controlled substance.
Document 6: Strengthening of FDA Integrity Act applies debarment provisions to any drug products, including new drugs and medical devices.
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to report on the implementation of debarment provisions.
Document 7: Medical Marijuana Patient Protection Act transfers marijuana from schedule I to schedule II of the Controlled Substances Act.
Allows physicians to prescribe marijuana for medical use in states where it is legal.
Document 8: Addiction Prevention and Responsible Opioid Practices Act addresses prescription opioid misuse and requires education programs for prescribers.
Establishes requirements for prescription drug monitoring systems to prevent over-prescribing.
Document 9: Reducing Unused Medications Act allows partial filling of prescriptions for controlled substances at the request of practitioners or patients.
Pharmacists must record the partial filling in the same manner as a full filling.
Document 10: Pediatric Medical Device Safety and Improvement Act requires applications for medical devices to include pediatric population considerations.
Requires postmarket surveillance on devices expected to have significant use in pediatric populations.

topic.name: Banking Regulation and Reform
topic.description: The documents collectively discuss various legislative acts aimed at regulating and reforming the banking industry to enhance financial stability and accountability.

1: Safe, Accountable, Fair, and Efficient Banking Act of 2010 or the SAFE Banking Act of 2010 - Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 to prohibit a bank holding company from holding more than 10% of the total amount of deposits of insured depository institutions in the United States.

Directs the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to require any bank holding company having a deposit concentration in violation of this Act to sell or transfer assets to unaffiliated firms to bring the company into compliance with this Act.
2: Directs the Comptroller General to study any bank holding company having more than $500 billion in consolidated assets with respect to favorable pricing of its debt relative to its risk profile resulting from the perception it will receive federal support in the event of any financial stress.
Defines 'economic benefit' as the difference between actual loan terms offered, debt or equity prices, or asset values and a reasonable estimate of what such terms, prices, or values might have been.
3: 21st Century Glass-Steagall Act of 2013 - Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to prohibit an insured depository institution from being or becoming an affiliate of any insurance company, securities entity, or swaps entity.

Prohibits any individual who is an officer, director, partner, or employee of any securities entity, insurance company, or swaps entity from serving simultaneously as an officer, director, employee, or other institution-affiliated party of any insured depository institution.
4: This bill amends the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 to subject a savings and loan holding company to registration requirements for securities whose issuer has total assets exceeding $10 million.
The Federal Deposit Insurance Act is amended to require the federal banking agencies to issue regulations allowing for a reduced reporting requirement for certain depository institutions.
5: This bill amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act to authorize the Financial Stability Oversight Council (FSOC) to subject a bank holding company to enhanced supervision and prudential standards.
FSOC must make these final determinations using an indicator-based measurement approach established by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision to determine systemic importance.
6: Return to Prudent Banking Act of 2013 - Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to prohibit an insured depository institution from being an affiliate of any broker or dealer, investment adviser, investment company.
Prohibits officers, directors and employees of securities firms from simultaneous service on the boards of depository institutions.
7: This bill cites circumstances under which, for purposes of determining capital requirements or measuring an insured depository institution's capital, such an institution may treat a non-accrual loan as an accrual loan.

An insured depository institution may treat a non-accrual loan as an accrual loan if the loan is current, and no monthly payment has been more than 30 days delinquent during the previous 6-month period.
8: 21st Century Glass-Steagall Act of 2013 - Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to prohibit an insured depository institution from being or becoming an affiliate of any insurance company, securities entity, or swaps entity.

Prohibits any individual who is an officer, director, partner, or employee of any securities entity, insurance company, or swaps entity from serving simultaneously as an officer, director, employee, or other institution-affiliated party of any insured depository institution.
9: Establishes a six-month moratorium during which the Financial Stability Oversight Council is prohibited from making a determination under the Financial Stability Act of 2010 that a U.S. nonbank financial company shall be supervised by the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System.
If the Council determines that material financial distress at the company could pose a threat to the financial stability of the United States.
10: Safe, Accountable, Fair, and Efficient Banking Act of 2012 or SAFE Banking Act of 2012 - Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 to prohibit a bank holding company from holding more than 10% of the total amount of deposits of insured depository institutions in the United States.

Directs the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to require any bank holding company having a deposit concentration in violation of specified interstate banking requirements to sell or transfer liabilities to unaffiliated firms.
